title: geum coccineum
intro: Geum coccineum, also known as the Scottish avens, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic effects. Geum coccineum has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, and has been used to treat conditions such as digestive issues, wounds, and skin irritations. The herbal preparations of Geum coccineum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, taken internally, or applied topically to the affected areas.
